demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The guy I slept with three weeks ago just texted me that he has Syphilis. I am absolutely terrified, my skin is crawling, and I need to be tested immediately.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A recent partner messaged tonight to say he was diagnosed with syphilis; the patient wants urgent STI testing and is in acute distress.

symptom_narrative.onset: the distress started tonight, within minutes of reading the partner's text message
symptom_narrative.duration: a few hours of intense anxiety this evening
symptom_narrative.character: overwhelming panic, racing heart, a crawling sensation on the skin, feeling terrified and unable to settle
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: says it is unbearable and they cannot stop pacing or crying
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: no physical pain anywhere — it is all fear and a racing heart
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on suddenly after the message and has not eased
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; wants to do something concrete like get tested
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about the exposure and worst-case outcomes
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no burning when passing urine, no discharge, no genital sores or ulcers, no rash, no fever
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no previous panic attacks like this described
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: too distressed to sleep or focus tonight, but fully independent and physically able

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: recent casual sexual encounter three weeks ago; reports it was unprotected. Profound worry about stigma and long-term health consequences.

objective_data_offered.value: appears very anxious and tearful, pacing, breathing fast, holding a phone with a message on screen
my heart is really racing and my skin feels like it's crawling
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported sensation during acute anxiety, not a measured vital sign
